dialogueChunks.intro: More bad news, {player_name}. It never stops.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The aftershocks opened a second fissure, and the ground beneath the emergency shelters is cracking. {target_npc} is evacuating families to the northern ridge, but {enemy_type} have been circling it all day. We need you to clear the ridge and gather more {quest_item} from the collapsed structures {location_hint}. Without materials, our efforts to {quest_action} stall completely.
dialogueChunks.outro: The ground won't stop shaking, and neither will the people. Hold us together a little longer, {player_name}.

playerResponses.text: I'll secure the ridge and bring back whatever I can salvage.
Crumbling ground and circling predators. Your settlement has the worst luck I've ever seen.
If the ground itself is giving way, no amount of supplies will help.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the relief supplies you brought? They weren't enough. Not even close.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We underestimated the damage from {quest_threat}. Two more buildings collapsed overnight. The medical supplies are buried in rubble. {target_npc} found an alternative source of {quest_item} in an old storehouse {location_hint}, but it's been sealed for years and the {enemy_type} have nested around it. We need those supplies to continue the effort to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: I keep thinking this is the worst of it, and then the next hour comes. {quest_stakes} are depending on us.

playerResponses.text: We're not done yet. Show me where the storehouse is.
More collapsed buildings and more monsters. This disaster has layers.
At some point, you need to accept what can't be saved.

dialogueChunks.intro: The situation has deteriorated, {player_name}. I won't sugarcoat it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} left contaminated mud in the remaining wells. People are getting sick from the only water available. {target_npc} says there's a clean spring {location_hint}, but the path is blocked by debris and overrun with {enemy_type}. We need clean water and {quest_item} to purify what's left, or disease will finish what the disaster started.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_threat} didn't end when the water stopped rising. It changed shape. And {quest_stakes} need us to {quest_action} before it gets worse.

playerResponses.text: Contaminated water on top of everything else? I'll find the spring.
Disease, debris, and monsters. A complete nightmare. Let's deal with it.
I'm not a healer or an engineer. This problem is bigger than me.

dialogueChunks.intro: I thought the worst was over. I was wrong, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Refugees arrived from a neighboring settlement hit even harder by the storm. They brought nothing but mouths that need feeding. {target_npc} couldn't turn them away, but now we have twice the people and half the supplies. Worse, the {enemy_type} followed them here, tracking by scent. We need more {quest_item} from the trading post {location_hint} and the {enemy_type} driven off. Our efforts to {quest_action} just doubled in scope.
dialogueChunks.outro: Too many people, too little of everything, and predators at the gates. {quest_stakes} need a miracle, and you're it.

playerResponses.text: Refugees and predators at the door? I'll handle the threat and bring back supplies.
One disaster feeding another. Fine, I'll sort out the immediate dangers.
There are limits to what one person can do, and I've reached mine.
